nativeRange.habitat: Marine rocky subtidal and intertidal zones; typically found attached to hard substrate in well-lit shallow reef environments

avoidWith: herbivorous fish likely to graze macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Thomas Irvine; 2048x1639. Single Portieria hornemannii frond held underwater against clean background — tack-sharp, vibrant red, delicate lace-like thallus fully in frame.
Brian du Preez; 689x1024. Single vivid-red frond, extremely sharp, clearly shows the finely branched lace-like structure and red pigment.
Brian du Preez; 1024x918. Three fronds showing dark-red feathery lace morphology; good detail, out-of-water on hand.
Philippe Bourjon; 1200x910. In-situ shot showing two red-brown clumps growing on reef substrate — represents natural growth habit.

summary: A finely branched ornamental red macroalgae with a delicate lace-like thallus, available in red, pink, orange, or yellow hues. Generally suited for experienced reef keepers as a display specimen.
careNotes: Elegant Lace (Portieria sp.) is a specialty red macroalgae — a member of the Rhodophyta, the large division of predominantly red-pigmented marine algae — in the family Rhizophyllidaceae. The most-studied member of the genus is Portieria hornemannii, also notable in scientific literature as the source of the antitumor compound halomon. In the aquarium, this species tends to do best under moderate full-spectrum lighting and moderate flow; excessive flow has been known to tear the delicate red thallus. Red macroalgae in this family generally should not be placed on the sand bed, as contact with fine substrate has been reported to cause melting at the base. Attachment is typically accomplished by wedging into rockwork or by loosely tying — super glue should be avoided, as it can cause tissue damage in delicate red species. Standard reef water quality is generally advised: nitrates 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm, with regular trace element supplementation including iron and iodine. Because this species has only recently appeared in the aquarium trade and captive data remain limited, husbandry guidance is largely extrapolated from family-level care practices and the needs of related red macroalgae.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source for this species. Product page confirms common name, scientific name (Portieria sp.), and lace-like morphology with red/pink/orange/yellow color variants.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care: The Basics
additional.notes: General macroalgae care guide used for water parameter ranges (temp 72–78°F / 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4) and red algae-specific husbandry caveats (no sand placement, avoid super glue).
